2018-12-01.title: ISIS Official Known for Caging Foes Is Captured by Iraq
2018-12-01.abstract: The captured operative was a midlevel official best known for having paraded captured Kurdish soldiers in cages.

2018-12-01.title: Canada Faces a New Challenge as a Century of Automaking Ends
2018-12-01.abstract: Both Prime Minister Justin Trudeau and Premier Doug Ford of Ontario pledged to help jobless G.M. workers in Canada. But what aid they can truly offer is unclear.

2018-12-01.title: North Korean Soldier Defects to South, Crossing Heavily Armed Border
2018-12-01.abstract: South Korean soldiers guided the defector to safety after finding him walking across the Demilitarized Zone, the South’s military said on Saturday.

2018-12-01.title: Rwandan Women Paddle Into the Male World of Fishing
2018-12-01.abstract: “Now a woman can say: ‘I can build a house by myself. I can look after my family properly. And even if my husband dies, we can live a better life.’”

2018-12-01.title: In a Divided Bosnia, Segregated Schools Persist
2018-12-01.abstract: Ethnic tensions are worsening in Bosnia two decades after a bloody, bitter war. The divide is most obvious inside the country’s segregated schools.

2018-12-02.title: Epic Battles, Palaces and Concubines: A Chinese Studio’s Vast World of Fantasy
2018-12-02.abstract: Hengdian World Studios, in a small town in central China, churns out scores of movies and television series from each period in the country’s history.

2018-12-02.title: Israeli Police Urge Bribery and Fraud Charges Against Netanyahu. Again.
2018-12-02.abstract: For the third time this year, the prime minister is accused of corruption. But this time, his governing coalition is already teetering.

2018-12-02.title: U.S. Airstrike Kills Senior Taliban Commander in Afghanistan
2018-12-02.abstract: Mullah Abdul Manan, who led the Taliban’s brutal campaigns in southern Afghanistan, was killed as American diplomats push the group to join peace talks.

2018-12-02.title: Killings of Journalists Show the Bloody Fingerprints of Organized Crime
2018-12-02.abstract: More than two dozen journalists worldwide have been killed by members of organized crime since the start of 2017, according to a report by Reporters Without Borders.

2018-12-02.title: Macron Returns to Turmoil, as France Weighs State of Emergency
2018-12-02.abstract: After a summit meeting in Argentina, President Emmanuel Macron surveyed the destruction left after the “Yellow Vest” protests.

2018-12-03.title: Far-Right Vox Party Erodes Socialists’ Control in Spain’s Largest Region
2018-12-03.abstract: Voting results in Andalusia were a blow to the country’s Socialist prime minister, who could be forced into a snap general election next year.

2018-12-03.title: Firms Drop Support for Cluster Bombs, but ‘Global Problem Is Much Worse’
2018-12-03.abstract: An advocacy group that wants to ban the munitions says an international treaty banning them is having an impact, though other companies continue to produce them.

2018-12-03.title: Qatar Says It Will Leave OPEC and Focus on Natural Gas
2018-12-03.abstract: The Persian Gulf state, a modest producer of oil, hinted that it wanted freedom from a cartel dominated by Saudi Arabia, a regional rival.

2018-12-03.title: Quiet Casualty of Sri Lanka’s Political Crisis: A Sugar Tax
2018-12-03.abstract: In an effort to increase public support for the disputed prime minister, the government is reducing a tax on sugary drinks that experts say is vital for the country’s health.

2018-12-03.title: Rodrigo Duterte Jokes About Marijuana Use, While Thousands Die in His Drug War
2018-12-03.abstract: The Philippine president’s joke that he uses marijuana to stay awake at official functions was criticized as insensitive.

2018-12-04.title: Mexico’s New President Restarts Investigation Into 43 Missing Students
2018-12-04.abstract: Just days after taking office President Andrés Manuel López Obrador created a commission to re-examine the case of 43 students who were attacked by the police in 2014.

2018-12-04.title: France’s Yellow Vest Protests: The Movement That Has Put Paris on Edge
2018-12-04.abstract: The grass-roots protests present President Emmanuel Macron with a potential political crisis. More mayhem may put his ability to govern into question.

2018-12-04.title: Saudis Disqualified from Hosting Chess Match After Israelis Complain
2018-12-04.abstract: The decision by the governing body for international chess is an image setback for the kingdom, which denied visas to Israeli players in 2017.

2018-12-04.title: Australia’s Liberal Party, Seeking to Project Stability, Tries to End Leadership Coups
2018-12-04.abstract: Internal power struggles and a revolving door to the post of prime minister have cost the governing Liberal Party at the polls.

2018-12-04.title: Israel Moves to Seal Hezbollah Tunnels at Lebanon Border
2018-12-04.abstract: The military action, called Operation Northern Shield, is aimed in part at limiting Iran’s regional ambitions, Israeli officials said.

2018-12-05.title: A Reclusive Nation Cracks Open Its Doors
2018-12-05.abstract: Eritrea’s 20-year war with neighboring Ethiopia is finally over. A journalist with roots in both nations visited to see if Eritrea can turn away from years of isolation.

2018-12-05.title: Lego Foundation and Sesame Street Team Up to Help Refugee Children
2018-12-05.abstract: A $100 million grant from the Lego Foundation will allow Sesame Workshop and aid groups to develop education programs for Syrian and Rohingya children.

2018-12-05.title: London Police’s Tactic to Stop Thieves on Scooters: Ram Them
2018-12-05.abstract: The police released a video showing officers hitting fleeing suspects on motorcycles and scooters, but though the prime minister backs the tactic, not everyone was pleased.

2018-12-05.title: To Feed U.S. Troops, Businessman Violated Iran Sanctions, Charges Say
2018-12-05.abstract: The federal government charged a Clinton supporter, Abul Huda Farouki of the Anham company, with defrauding it by shipping to Afghanistan through Iran.

2018-12-05.title: How France’s ‘Yellow Vests’ Differ From Populist Movements Elsewhere
2018-12-05.abstract: These protests are more akin to the anti-Wall Street than to the right-wing nationalism seen elsewhere.

2018-12-06.title: Five U.S. Marines Missing in Aircraft Accident Off Japan
2018-12-06.abstract: Japanese and American aircraft are searching the waters off Japan. Two people have been found.

2018-12-06.title: With Chris Dawson’s Arrest in Sydney Killing, Others Break Silence on Teacher Misconduct
2018-12-06.abstract: A hit podcast has led to an arrest and broken the silence on a culture in which teachers exploited their positions to enter into sexual relationships with students.

2018-12-06.title: ‘Already an Exception’: Merkel’s Legacy Is Shaped by Migration and Austerity
2018-12-06.abstract: As the German chancellor’s party chooses a new leader, the values she embodies are in danger. Some ask whether her choices helped plant the seeds of the forces now tearing Europe apart.

2018-12-06.title: North Korea Is Expanding Missile Base With Eye Toward U.S., Experts Warn
2018-12-06.abstract: A new study says that North Korea is expanding a base that would be one of the most likely sites for deploying intercontinental ballistic missiles capable of reaching the United States.

2018-12-06.title: Iceland in Uproar Over Secret Recording of Politicians’ Sexist and Crude Remarks
2018-12-06.abstract: A bar patron recorded incendiary remarks that the group made about female colleagues, then sent them to the news media. Now many Icelanders want the politicians to resign.

2018-12-07.title: In Blow to Haley, U.N. Rejects Measure Condemning Hamas
2018-12-07.abstract: An effort by Nikki R. Haley, the American ambassador, to counter what she called an anti-Israel bias at the United Nations became, in her words, “just another ordinary day.”

2018-12-07.title: Riots Flare in Athens on the 10th Anniversary of a Police Killing
2018-12-07.abstract: Young people with homemade firebombs and riot police with tear gas clashed in the streets of Athens and Thessaloniki on Thursday, after protests over a police shooting unraveled.

2018-12-07.title: China Watchers Demand Action on Harassment of New Zealand Professor
2018-12-07.abstract: A letter signed by dozens of China experts demands that the New Zealand government protect a professor who said she had been harassed over her research critical of China.

2018-12-07.title: Angela Merkel’s Political Life in Pictures: Tracing the End of an Era
2018-12-07.abstract: As Germany’s chancellor enters the final chapter of a long career, the snapshots of her political life chronicle an era that may end with her.

2018-12-07.title: The ‘It’ ’80s Party Girl Is Now a Defender of the Catholic Faith
2018-12-07.abstract: Princess Gloria von Thurn und Taxis used to hang with Warhol. Now, she hosts Stephen K. Bannon and the bishops opposed to Pope Francis in her 500-room palace.

2018-12-08.title: Justin Trudeau Is Facing a Carbon Tax Backlash. He’s Not Alone.
2018-12-08.abstract: Prime Minister Justin Trudeau has made carbon pricing a keystone of his political program for Canada, but critics are multiplying, at home and abroad

2018-12-08.title: Nightclub Stampede in Italy Leaves 6 Dead and Dozens Injured
2018-12-08.abstract: Panic broke out after midnight Saturday at the club in the small town of Corinaldo, where a popular rapper was scheduled to perform.

2018-12-08.title: As Winter Arrives, Thousands of Migrants Are Trapped in Bosnia
2018-12-08.abstract: Migrants sheltering in Bosnia report widespread violence by the border police in Croatia as Europe pushes to secure its frontiers.

2018-12-08.title: A City in Spain Plans to Exile 5,000 Pigeons. Will They Stay Away?
2018-12-08.abstract: Cádiz, Spain, will capture and move the birds hundreds of miles away after a complaint that the birds are driving away tourists. Officials hope it’s a one-way trip.

2018-12-08.title: French Police Crack Down on ‘Yellow Vests’ With Tear Gas and Over a Thousand Arrests
2018-12-08.abstract: The police took a more aggressive stance this weekend as more hard-core elements hijacked the demonstrations. They arrested nearly 1,400 people.

2018-12-09.title: Police in Australia Offer Million-Dollar Reward in Gay Man’s 1988 Death
2018-12-09.abstract: The police hope the reward will lead to information about the death of Scott Johnson, an American mathematician who was forced off a cliff in Sydney in a hate crime.

2018-12-09.title: New Zealand Police Find Body in Search for Grace Millane, a British Backpacker
2018-12-09.abstract: The police say a body was found on the outskirts of Auckland in their search for Ms. Millane, who vanished a week ago on the night before her 22nd birthday.

2018-12-09.title: Life in Tijuana Means Negotiating ‘La Línea,’ an Always Present Wall
2018-12-09.abstract: Daily life in Tijuana, Mexico, is defined less by the border wall as an impenetrable obstacle than by the ebb and flow of movement across it.

2018-12-09.title: With Brexit Vote Looming, Britons on Both Sides Rally in London
2018-12-09.abstract: Protesters from Britain’s right and left took to the streets on Sunday, offering starkly different visions of the country’s future as the government scrambled to salvage its plan for exiting the European Union.

2018-12-09.title: Theresa May Tried to Lead Britain to a Brexit Compromise. Was It Too Late?
2018-12-09.abstract: Devoid of bombast and uninterested in personal power, she could have been the perfect messenger. But an early focus on satisfying hard-liners might have been her undoing.

2018-12-10.title: Understanding the Collective Rage of France’s ‘Gilets Jaunes’
2018-12-10.abstract: Shuttered shops, burning cars and tear gas. We were on the ground in Paris with the “Yellow Vests” protesters as they took to the streets for the fourth week in a row.

2018-12-10.title: Man Accused of Killing British Backpacker Grace Millane Appears in New Zealand Court
2018-12-10.abstract: “Your grief must be desperate,” a judge told the victim’s family. Ms. Millane vanished just before her 22nd birthday while on a trip around the world.

2018-12-10.title: Will Kim Jong-un Visit Seoul This Month? South Korea Is Holding Its Breath
2018-12-10.abstract: South Korea’s president, Moon Jae-in, said a visit by Kim Jong-un to Seoul could happen this month. But Mr. Kim has yet to accept the invitation.

2018-12-10.title: Australia’s Migrant Camps Are ‘Crime Against Humanity,’ Lawsuit Claims
2018-12-10.abstract: More than 1,000 migrants held in offshore detention centers said the government had abrogated its “duty of care,” as required by international law.

2018-12-10.title: Documenting the Disappearing Glaciers of Iceland
2018-12-10.abstract: On foot and by plane, Ragnar Axelsson is photographing the glaciers that cover his homeland, creating a poetic record of the structures for a future without them.

2018-12-11.title: A Member of Britain’s Parliament Seized the Ceremonial Mace, and Confusion Reigned
2018-12-11.abstract: The act of protest by a member of the House of Commons drew attention to the symbolic 17th-century object, and to the chaotic state of British politics.

2018-12-11.title: Five Marines Declared Dead After Aviation Disaster
2018-12-11.abstract: The Marines were declared dead six days after two American military planes crashed off the coast of Japan during a routine training exercise.

2018-12-11.title: U.S. Returns Bells Taken as War Booty From Philippines in 1901
2018-12-11.abstract: American troops took the three church bells after avenging a deadly attack on their fellow soldiers, and the seizure has complicated ties with the Philippines since.

2018-12-11.title: A Hotline for the Broken-Hearted and the Poison-in-Hand
2018-12-11.abstract: Everyday Sri Lankans — a retired banker, a former nurse, a marriage counselor — volunteer at a phone counseling line to ease burdens in a war-traumatized country.

2018-12-11.title: Thai Court Holds Bahraini Soccer Star Who Escaped Persecution
2018-12-11.abstract: The Bahraini man, Hakeem al-Araibi, had been granted refugee status in Australia, but was detained in Thailand after a request from his home country to extradite him.

2018-12-12.title: Argentina Convicts Ex-Ford Executives for Abuses During Dictatorship
2018-12-12.abstract: Two former executives of Ford Motor were convicted of helping the country’s military dictators kidnap and torture 24 workers during the 1970s.

2018-12-12.title: Dozens More Cambodian Immigrants to Be Deported From U.S., Officials Say
2018-12-12.abstract: A new group of more than 40 deportees, many of them the children of refugees, will be the largest yet to be ejected by the Trump administration.

2018-12-12.title: China Says Detained Canadian Worked for Group Without Legal Registration
2018-12-12.abstract: The assertion was Beijing’s first official comment on the detention of Michael Kovrig, a former diplomat now working for a nongovernmental organization.

2018-12-12.title: The Foreign Billionaires, Activists and Missionaries Detained in China
2018-12-12.abstract: China has a long history of arresting or holding foreigners for mysterious reasons, often in a tit-for-tat play to put pressure on overseas rivals.

2018-12-12.title: Theresa May Fights No-Confidence Vote Amid Brexit Disarray
2018-12-12.abstract: The U.K. prime minister promised to contest the ballot of her party’s lawmakers, scheduled for Wednesday evening, “with everything I’ve got.”

2018-12-13.title: Caught Between Feuding Giants, Canada Fears Consequences
2018-12-13.abstract: Fulfilling an extradition request from its ally and neighbor has left Canada vulnerable to actions by China, an increasingly important export market.

2018-12-13.title: Ukraine Court Rules Manafort Disclosure Caused ‘Meddling’ in U.S. Election
2018-12-13.abstract: A prosecutor and a lawmaker broke the law by revealing details about possibly illegal payments earmarked for Paul Manafort, which “resulted in meddling” in the 2016 election, a court in Kiev said.

2018-12-13.title: Second Canadian Arrested in China, Escalating Diplomatic Feud
2018-12-13.abstract: China confirmed the arrest of Michael Spavor, an entrepreneur, and accused him and another Canadian of unspecified crimes undermining China’s national security.

2018-12-13.title: Jailing Hundreds of Journalists Worldwide Is the ‘New Normal,’ Group Finds
2018-12-13.abstract: The Committee to Protect Journalists, an advocacy group, said more than 250 journalists were imprisoned worldwide for the third consecutive year.

2018-12-13.title: Clashes Over Trade and Detentions: What China, the U.S. and Canada Are Fighting Over
2018-12-13.abstract: While the trade war between the United States and China has been put on hold, new disputes over a series of detentions have raised alarm on both sides of the Pacific.

2018-12-14.title: Who’s in the Middle of a Spat Between the U.S. and China? Canada
2018-12-14.abstract: Canada is in a tricky spot, boxed in the middle between its two largest trading partners, and worried about having to choose sides.

2018-12-14.title: Acclaimed Photographer Arrested in Far West China, Wife Says
2018-12-14.abstract: Lu Guang disappeared early last month while visiting the sensitive Xinjiang region where hundreds of thousands of Muslims are held in internment camps. Now the police have confirmed his arrest.

2018-12-14.title: Just 2 of More Than 480 Yemenis Receive Refugee Status in South Korea
2018-12-14.abstract: The sudden arrival of asylum seekers fleeing war in their homeland set off a wave of anti-immigrant sentiment in South Korea.

2018-12-14.title: Montreal Turns to Coyote Hazing After 19 People Are Bitten
2018-12-14.abstract: As coyotes have flourished in urban environments, cities are faced with the task of learning how to coexist peacefully.

2018-12-14.title: Strasbourg Suspect Was on a Watch List: What Are the ‘S Files’?
2018-12-14.abstract: Chérif Chekatt, 29 was flagged by French security services for possible radicalization and put in a database. But that does not mean he was monitored around the clock.

2018-12-15.title: The Fight for and Against Australian Secrecy
2018-12-15.abstract: This week’s Australia Letter would like to tell you more, a lot more, but it can’t.

2018-12-15.title: Australia Recognizes West Jerusalem as Capital of Israel
2018-12-15.abstract: The prime minister formally recognized West Jerusalem as the capital of Israel, but said Australia would not move its embassy until a peace settlement is reached.

2018-12-15.title: Brazil’s Violent Drug Trade Overruns Paraguay: ‘Scenes You Only See in Movies’
2018-12-15.abstract: Some of Brazil’s most powerful gangs have established a foothold in neighboring Paraguay, importing havoc as they export guns and drugs. One man is at the center of much of the mayhem.

2018-12-15.title: Virgin of Guadalupe Is ‘No. 1 Mother’ in Mexico, a Binding Force Across Divides
2018-12-15.abstract: It is hard to overstate the singular importance of the Virgin of Guadalupe to the Mexican identity. Nowhere is the country’s devotion more apparent than at a yearly pilgrimage.

2018-12-15.title: ‘Yellow Vest’ Protests Dwindle Amid Warnings and Concessions
2018-12-15.abstract: Thousands still turned out after the government cautioned that protests could complicate the task of preventing terrorism after an attack this week in Strasbourg.

2018-12-16.title: Where Reindeer Are a Way of Life
2018-12-16.abstract: The indigenous Sami people have fought for generations to preserve their identity. The latest battles are against Norway’s limits on reindeer herds.

2018-12-16.title: Lawyer’s Status Throws Genocide Conviction of Khmer Rouge Leader Into Doubt
2018-12-16.abstract: An international tribunal dismissed a Dutch lawyer who had defended one of Khmer Rouge leaders. The lawyer’s bar membership lapsed in 2016.

2018-12-16.title: Egypt Unearths Tomb of Royal Priest From 4,400 Years Ago
2018-12-16.abstract: The well-preserved tomb was discovered in Saqqara, an ancient city south of Cairo. Officials called it “one of a kind.”

2018-12-16.title: Hard-Line U.S. Tactics Will ‘Block’ Path to Denuclearization, North Korea Warns
2018-12-16.abstract: North Korea said that if Washington continued to escalate its sanctions and rights campaign, it could shatter any chance of denuclearizing the country.

2018-12-16.title: China’s Detention Camps for Muslims Turn to Forced Labor
2018-12-16.abstract: Mounting evidence suggests ethnic minorities caught in the mass internment drive in Xinjiang are being assigned to new factories making clothes.

2018-12-17.title: France’s Far Right Sees Gold in Yellow Vest Movement
2018-12-17.abstract: National Front leaders are trying to turn protests that have left much of France in upheaval to their purposes. But it may be a double-edged sword.

2018-12-17.title: Explosion in Japan Injures Dozens in Crowded Pub
2018-12-17.abstract: The blast in Sapporo was powerful enough to shatter windows across the street.

2018-12-17.title: How an Elite Nigerian Unit Killed Dozens of Protesters
2018-12-17.abstract: Nigeria’s military said it acted in self-defense. Our visual investigation shows that this claim doesn’t hold up, and reveals how an elite military unit used machine guns on unarmed marchers in October, killing dozens.

2018-12-17.title: Nigeria Says Soldiers Who Killed Marchers Were Provoked. Video Shows Otherwise.
2018-12-17.abstract: A close review of video from the largest and most deadly of the protests, as well as interviews with witnesses, clearly shows the military opening fire on unarmed demonstrators.

2018-12-17.title: Bombing Near Athens Damages Offices of Greek Media Group
2018-12-17.abstract: A caller warned of the explosion 45 minutes in advance, so no one was hurt. Greece has experienced several similar attacks by domestic groups in recent years.

2018-12-18.title: New Zealand Says Voters Will Decide Whether Cannabis Is Legalized
2018-12-18.abstract: With a referendum planned for 2020, New Zealand apparently will be the first country to put legalizing recreational marijuana to a public vote.

2018-12-18.title: China’s Leader Says Party Must Control ‘All Tasks,’ and Asian Markets Slump
2018-12-18.abstract: The Chinese president marked 40 years of economic liberalization by arguing that the Communist Party’s firm control must not waver.

2018-12-18.title: What Next for France’s ‘Yellow Vests’?
2018-12-18.abstract: For five weeks, demonstrators have marched in Paris and beyond, staging a nationwide rebellion against President Emmanuel Macron. We went to the French countryside, where the movement began, to find out whether the leaderless campaign could win longstanding changes.

2018-12-18.title: North Korea, Fearing K-Pop and Porn, Warns Against Smartphones’ Influence
2018-12-18.abstract: Mobile phones “instill students with unhealthy ideology,” said a state-run paper in the North, where many users have found ways around official information controls.

2018-12-18.title: Kashmiri Teenagers Are Dying to Protect Militants
2018-12-18.abstract: Indian forces hunting for separatist fighters now regularly face angry crowds seeking to defend them. Scores of civilians have been killed.

2018-12-19.title: Ukraine Wins New I.M.F. Support as Tensions Rise With Russia
2018-12-19.abstract: The International Monetary Fund agreed to a $3.9 billion line of credit for Ukraine, whose economy has struggled with corruption and a conflict with Russia.

2018-12-19.title: In Home Village of Girl Who Died in U.S. Custody, Poverty Drives Migration
2018-12-19.abstract: Jakelin Caal Maquin, a 7-year-old girl who died in the custody of American officials, came from a poor Guatemalan village where many see emigration as the only escape from poverty.

2018-12-19.title: Not Just Khashoggi: Reprisal Killings of Journalists Surged This Year
2018-12-19.abstract: At least 53 journalists were killed worldwide in 2018, most of them targeted because of their work, the Committee to Protect Journalists said on Wednesday.

2018-12-19.title: Chinese City Bans Christmas Displays Amid Religious Crackdown
2018-12-19.abstract: In this Chinese city, the War on Christmas is over. And Christmas lost.

2018-12-19.title: That Punishing Blockade? ‘We’ve Moved On,’ Qatar Says
2018-12-19.abstract: After its larger neighbors began a blockade of the tiny Gulf state, Qatar retooled its economy, beefed up its military and adjusted to the new reality.

2018-12-20.title: Der Spiegel Fires Award-Winning Writer, Citing Fabrication on ‘Grand Scale’
2018-12-20.abstract: Claas Relotius, a writer for the German magazine Der Spiegel, was fired after he admitted inventing quotations and characters in at least 14 articles.

2018-12-20.title: Six Decapitated Seal Pups Found on New Zealand Beach
2018-12-20.abstract: Officials said people had hurt or killed seals before, in the mistaken belief that they are responsible for low fish stocks.

2018-12-20.title: Dutch Consider Taking Russia to Court Over Downing of Jet Over Ukraine
2018-12-20.abstract: In a letter, the Netherlands’ foreign minister said that Russia had rebuffed talks about a possible settlement for the victims of a civilian plane shot down over Ukraine.

2018-12-20.title: Gatwick Airport Shut Down by ‘Deliberate’ Drone Incursions
2018-12-20.abstract: The closure, which affected tens of thousands of travelers, shows how vulnerable airports have become to devices readily available in hobbyist shops.

2018-12-20.title: Chinese Authorities Identify Third Canadian Detained in Diplomatic Spat
2018-12-20.abstract: A teacher from Alberta, Sarah D. McIver, is the latest Canadian to be detained by China, which appears to be pressuring Canada to release a Chinese executive.

2018-12-21.title: Winners and Losers in Trump’s Planned Troop Withdrawal From Syria
2018-12-21.abstract: President Trump's planned withdrawal of troops from Syria risks upsetting the fragile balance of power in a complex, multi-sided war.

2018-12-21.title: Syria Pullout by U.S. Tilts Mideast Toward Iran and Russia, Isolating Israel
2018-12-21.abstract: President Trump’s decision to withdraw American troops from Syria clears the way for Iran to expand its influence and leaves Israel alone to stop it.

2018-12-21.title: Mattis Resignation and Afghan News Alarm U.S. Allies
2018-12-21.abstract: In his parting letter, Mr. Mattis warned that America must “treat allies with respect” to preserve world peace.

2018-12-21.title: Trump Signs Law Punishing Chinese Officials Who Restrict Access to Tibet
2018-12-21.abstract: Lawmakers hope to force China to lift the travel restrictions, which they say are meant to conceal abuses of Tibetans’ human rights.

2018-12-21.title: Two Arrests, and Many Questions, as Gatwick Reopens After Drone Threat
2018-12-21.abstract: What was behind the incursions? And why couldn’t the British authorities stopped them more quickly?

2018-12-22.title: What Is Hungary’s ‘Slave Law,’ and Why Has It Provoked Opposition?
2018-12-22.abstract: Viktor Orban’s government has faced a united opposition and unusually sustained street protests after it increased the cap for overtime to 400 hours a year.

2018-12-22.title: At 14, She Hunts Wolves and Takes Selfies With Cherished Eagle in Mongolia
2018-12-22.abstract: In western Mongolia, hunting for fur and food with trained eagles is an important part of the local culture — and a way for tech-savvy teenagers to connect with their elders.

2018-12-22.title: Video of Tourist’s Killing in Morocco Is Most Likely Real, Norway Says
2018-12-22.abstract: Nine more people have been detained in connection with the killings of Maren Ueland, 28, of Norway and Louisa Vesterager Jespersen, 24, of Denmark.

2018-12-22.title: Philippine Lawmaker, a Duterte Ally, Is Killed in Pre-Election Violence
2018-12-22.abstract: Rodel M. Batocabe, 52, was fatally shot during a gift-giving ceremony on Saturday. He was the 21st official killed since President Rodrigo Duterte took office.

2018-12-22.title: Married Couple Arrested Over Drone Incursions at Gatwick Airport
2018-12-22.abstract: The man, 47, and woman, 54, were being held on suspicion of disrupting civil aviation services after three days of chaos at Britain’s second-largest airport.

2018-12-23.title: Tsunami in Indonesia Kills Over 280, With No Warning or Quake
2018-12-23.abstract: Officials said volcanic activity on Anak Krakatau island might have started the surge, which injured at least 1,000 people.

2018-12-23.title: ‘Damaged’ Drone Found Near Gatwick; Arrested Couple Are Freed Without Charge
2018-12-23.abstract: The drone was being forensically examined, and a police chief said two people held for questioning were “no longer suspects in the drone incidents at Gatwick.”

2018-12-23.title: How Volcanic Activity Can Spawn Tsunamis
2018-12-23.abstract: No earthquakes were recorded before the tsunami struck, the Indonesian authorities said. But there had been an eruption on the volcanic island of Anak Krakatau about half an hour before.

2018-12-23.title: Santa Arrested in Iraq? Social Media Says Yes, Police Say No
2018-12-23.abstract: The Iraqi police said that a widely shared photo had been taken not in Iraq but in Syria, and that the officers in the picture with the Santa were helping distribute presents to children.

2018-12-23.title: Der Spiegel to Press Charges Against Reporter Who Made Up Articles
2018-12-23.abstract: The United States ambassador to Germany waded into the scandal by accusing the newsmagazine of anti-American bias and calling for an independent audit.

2018-12-24.title: Praise for English Fizz Provokes Splutters in Champagne
2018-12-24.abstract: British bubbly makers are finding their prospects improved by climate change. Some French competitors denounce it as “pure propaganda,” as others buy English land.

2018-12-24.title: It’s Cold, Dark and Lacks Parking. But Is This Finnish Town the World’s Happiest?
2018-12-24.abstract: Polling suggests that Kauniainen, a small suburb of the Finnish capital, is the most satisfied town in the world’s happiest country. The secret may be the wealth of state-funded services.

2018-12-24.title: Yuan Mu, Face of China’s Tiananmen Square Crackdown, Dies at 90
2018-12-24.abstract: Mr. Yuan is remembered as the government spokesman who said that “not one person” died in the 1989 assault on protesters. Hundreds were reported killed.

2018-12-24.title: 1918: President Wilson Gets Lost in Paris
2018-12-24.abstract: Visiting France in the month after victory in World War I, President Woodrow Wilson and his wife found themselves needing directions from two boys in the street.

2018-12-24.title: In Nicaragua, Ortega Was on the Ropes. Now, He Has Protesters on the Run.
2018-12-24.abstract: When huge protests demanded that he leave office, President Daniel Ortega’s days seemed numbered. But his repressive response has sent thousands into hiding and tightened his hold on power.

2018-12-25.title: Christmas in Lebanon: ‘Jesus Isn’t Only for the Christians’
2018-12-25.abstract: Few seasons frame the everyday give-and-take of religious coexistence quite like Christmastime in Lebanon, where hijab-wearing women snap selfies in front of Beirut’s colossal Christmas tree.

2018-12-25.title: Mexico Helicopter Crash Kills Governor and Her Husband, an Ex-Governor
2018-12-25.abstract: Martha Erika Alonso, the governor of Puebla, and Rafael Moreno Valle, who once led the same state, were among at least five people who died.

2018-12-25.title: As China Cracks Down on Churches, Christians Declare ‘We Will Not Forfeit Our Faith’
2018-12-25.abstract: China this year banned online sales of the Bible, burned crosses, demolished churches and forced at least a half-dozen places of worship to close.

2018-12-25.title: On the Surface, Hungary Is a Democracy. But What Lies Underneath?
2018-12-25.abstract: Hungary has the trappings of a 21st-century European democracy, but uses its devices to exert the same kind of control as the autocracies of the Cold War.

2018-12-25.title: Pope Francis, in Christmas Speech, Emphasizes ‘Fraternity’
2018-12-25.abstract: In his “Urbi et Orbi” (“To the City and to the World”) benediction, the pontiff declares that differences are a source of richness rather than danger.

2018-12-26.title: Japan to Resume Commercial Whaling, Defying International Ban
2018-12-26.abstract: The country says it will stop its annual hunts in the Antarctic — purportedly carried out for research purposes — and restrict its whalers to Japanese waters.

2018-12-26.title: Thailand to Allow Medical Marijuana, a First in Southeast Asia
2018-12-26.abstract: Lawmakers unanimously approved legislation that would allow the use of cannabis under medical supervision. The penalty for recreational use would remain in place.

2018-12-26.title: In 12 Minutes, Everything Went Wrong
2018-12-26.abstract: How the pilots of Lion Air Flight 610 lost control.

2018-12-26.title: China Holds Secret Trial for Rights Lawyer After 3 Years in Detention
2018-12-26.abstract: The case of Wang Quanzhang shows how Chinese officials can override legal protections. But he and his wife have also become symbols of resistance.

2018-12-26.title: Fake Islands Bring a Dutch Lake Back to Life
2018-12-26.abstract: More than 40 years after the Netherlands caused an environmental calamity, an ambitious if costly solution is showing signs of enormous promise.

2018-12-27.title: Chinese Official Tied to Billionaire Fugitive Is Sentenced to Life in Prison
2018-12-27.abstract: The sentencing of Ma Jian was the latest sign that President Xi Jinping has no intention of easing his signature anticorruption drive.

2018-12-27.title: India Struggles to Save 15 Trapped Coal Miners
2018-12-27.abstract: The workers have been trapped for two weeks, their chances of survival increasingly slim, local officials in the northeastern state of Meghalaya say.

2018-12-27.title: Gatwick Airport Drone: Lots of Second-Guessing, but Not Many Answers
2018-12-27.abstract: After the London airport was closed for almost 36 hours last week, officials are calling for new investigators, critiquing government readiness and suggesting new solutions.

2018-12-27.title: Frenchman, 71, Aims to Cross the Atlantic in a Giant Barrel
2018-12-27.abstract: Jean-Jacques Savin set off from the Canary Islands this week for the Caribbean, propelled only by currents and winds, in an orange capsule he built himself.

2018-12-27.title: Saudi King Shakes Up Cabinet, Keeping Power in Son’s Hands
2018-12-27.abstract: Allies of the crown prince, Mohammed bin Salman, were among those promoted, despite the war in Yemen and the death of the dissident columnist Jamal Khashoggi.

2018-12-28.title: Angered at Trump’s Visit, Some Iraqi Lawmakers Want U.S. Troops Out
2018-12-28.abstract: A range of Iraqi politicians criticized President Trump over his surprise visit as an affront. The most strident critics were affiliated with Moktada al-Sadr, a nationalist Shiite cleric.

2018-12-28.title: Saudi Strikes, American Bombs, Yemeni Suffering
2018-12-28.abstract: How Saudi Arabia’s war tactics have fueled Yemen’s humanitarian crisis.

2018-12-28.title: Along a Road of Sorrow, a Toddler Lost Her Name, Her Family and Her Life
2018-12-28.abstract: A roadside bomb wiped out a family in Afghanistan, except for a tiny girl who arrived in a shepherd’s arms at a hospital where no one knew her name. She was called “Angel” as she struggled to survive.

2018-12-28.title: Syria’s Kurds, Feeling Betrayed by the U.S., Ask Assad Government for Protection
2018-12-28.abstract: Other powers in Syria have begun positioning themselves to take advantage of the vacuum left by a U.S. withdrawal.

2018-12-28.title: He Helped Indonesia Through a ‘Year of Disasters,’ While Facing His Own
2018-12-28.abstract: Sutopo Purwo Nugroho, a government spokesman, has kept the public informed about earthquakes, landslides and tsunamis while being treated for Stage 4 lung cancer.

2018-12-29.title: Chinese Scientist Who Claimed to Make Genetically Edited Babies Is Kept Under Guard
2018-12-29.abstract: He Jiankui, who was last seen in public in November soon after his stunning announcement, is sequestered in a guesthouse in the city of Shenzhen.

2018-12-29.title: Stalin’s Soaring Moscow Towers Sorely Need Body Work
2018-12-29.abstract: The massive “Stalin high-rises,” built to embody the victorious spirit of postwar Russia, still offer some of the best apartments in Moscow, but are now badly decayed.

2018-12-29.title: China to Retry Canadian on Drug Charge Amid Diplomatic Spat
2018-12-29.abstract: Robert Lloyd Schellenberg appealed against his jail sentence in a previously obscure case. He now faces a retrial that could impose a death penalty.

2018-12-29.title: Egypt Kills 40 Suspected Militants After Deadly Bombing of Tour Bus
2018-12-29.abstract: Security forces raided sites in North Sinai and Giza, the Interior Ministry said on Saturday, a day after a bomb killed three Vietnamese tourists and their Egyptian guide.

2018-12-29.title: Hungary Sheltered a Fugitive Prime Minister. Did It Help Him Escape?
2018-12-29.abstract: After a former Macedonian prime minister jumped bail and ended up in Hungary, the far-right Hungarian prime minister, Viktor Orban, was accused of helping him escape.

2018-12-30.title: An Israeli Soldier Killed a Medic in Gaza. We Investigated the Fatal Shot.
2018-12-30.abstract: On June 1, an Israeli soldier shot into a crowd, killing a volunteer medic named Rouzan al-Najjar. Israeli officials say soldiers only use live fire as a last resort. Our investigation shows otherwise. We analyzed over 1,000 photos and videos, froze the fatal moment in a 3-D model of the protest, and interviewed more than 30 witnesses and I.D.F. commanders to reveal how Rouzan was killed.

2018-12-30.title: A Day, a Life: When a Medic Was Killed in Gaza, Was It an Accident?
2018-12-30.abstract: When an Israeli soldier killed Rouzan al-Najjar, Palestinians called her an innocent martyr. Israel portrayed her as a threat. The truth is more complicated.

2018-12-30.title: Hello. Does Poland Have Vampires? My Date Wants to Know My Blood Type.
2018-12-30.abstract: The British Foreign Office released a list of the oddest phone calls it received this year from Britons abroad.

2018-12-30.title: China Thwarts U.S. Effort to Promote American Culture on Campuses
2018-12-30.abstract: The Chinese authorities blocked activities of centers run by American universities and financed by the State Department. After 10 years, none are left.

2018-12-30.title: Romania, Fighting the E.U., Prepares to Lead It
2018-12-30.abstract: The government has turned to Euroskeptic rhetoric to defend its stance on anticorruption legislation, even as it is scheduled to take its turn in the presidency of the bloc as Brexit looms.

2018-12-31.title: Philippine President Says He Sexually Abused Housemaid as a Teenager
2018-12-31.abstract: A spokesman for President Rodgrigo Duterte said later that the remarks were intended as a joke to expose the hypocrisy of the Roman Catholic Church.

2018-12-31.title: Year-End Storm in the Philippines Kills More Than 60
2018-12-31.abstract: Officials said the storm known as Usman, which struck the region of Bicol on Saturday, had left at least 61 dead, while 18 others were missing.

2018-12-31.title: C.I.A.’s Afghan Forces Leave a Trail of Abuse and Anger
2018-12-31.abstract: The fighters hold the line in the war’s toughest spots, but officials say their brutal tactics are terrorizing the public and undermining the U.S. mission.

2018-12-31.title: 7 Dead and Dozens Missing in Russia Building Collapse
2018-12-31.abstract: A gas leak was thought to be the cause of the early-morning blast that brought down a residential apartment building in central Russia.

2018-12-31.title: Bangladesh’s Leader Wins a Third Term but Opposition Contests Results
2018-12-31.abstract: In a vote with widespread irregularities and intimidation, Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina remains in power, but faces accusations that she’s more autocratic.
